request.lastMessagePreview: I'd like to set up a new campaign.

result.text:
The table wakes.
A little universe unfolds its corners in the dark.
The dice are still asleep, the maps are blank, and somewhere beyond the curtain a thousand possible worlds are holding their breath. We can build anything here: a haunted mining town, a doomed starship, a kingdom with a knife under its crown, a coffee shop where reality politely waits in line. I’ll be your master of ceremonies for the opening rites — asking only what matters, hiding the scaffolding, and letting the world grow around your answers. Before we begin — what should I call you?

result.text: Excellent, Sam. Adult table, Full Setup, FATE Accelerated, rules behind the curtain, The Chronicler at the helm. The engine has heard you. Pulp planar adventure is a splendid phrase — it has brass goggles, impossible horizons, stolen maps, cliffhanger escapes, and a villain with at least one monologue prepared in case of thunder. Before we put a name to the expedition or give your hero a shadow on the page, one quick switch:

input.prompt: Do you want images in your game? (you can change this in game settings later)

A sky-courier smuggling refugees off worlds marked for erasure gives us our engine; a blood-debt to the tyrant doing the erasing gives us the knife in the gears. You’ve also handed me permission to light the fuses myself: heroic pulp, one-shot, FATE Accelerated behind the curtain. result.text:
The Atlas of Burning Doors
A pulpy planar-adventure / heroic pulp one-shot, at Balanced difficulty, narrated by The Chronicler. Images are on. We’ll use FATE Accelerated, but I’ll run the rules behind the curtain — you won’t need to track dice, aspects, fate points, or mechanical spends unless you ask. You’ll be playing Roan Calloway, a quick-witted sky-courier who smuggles refugees off worlds marked for erasure; his recurring trouble is a blood-debt to the very tyrant whose work he’s undoing. For the FATE skeleton, I’ll set:
High Concept: Quick-Witted Sky-Courier of the Planar Routes
Trouble: Blood-Debt to the Tyrant Whose Work I’m Undoing
Extra Aspect: No Door Is Closed If You Hit It Fast Enough
Approaches: Flashy +3; Quick +2, Clever +2; Sneaky +1, Careful +1; Forceful +0
Style: cliffhangers, impossible skies, desperate rescues, tyrannical cosmic bureaucracy, and a few lovingly ridiculous pulp hazards. The campaign premise: The tyrant called the Red Auditor has begun stamping whole worlds for erasure, collapsing them out of the planar sky one by one. Roan Calloway has stolen refugees from beneath that stamp before — but tonight, a doomed world sends him a distress signal addressed in his own blood, and the only route out runs through a forbidden door-map that everyone sensible believes was burned centuries ago. Sound good, Sam — shall I seal it and open the first door?

request.lastMessagePreview: Yes, that all sounds perfect — please call finalize_setup now with sensible defaults for anything unspecified, and let's

input.campaign_premise: The tyrant called the Red Auditor has begun stamping whole worlds for erasure, collapsing them out of the planar sky one by one. Roan Calloway has stolen refugees from beneath that stamp before — but tonight, a doomed world sends him a distress signal addressed in his own blood, and the only route out runs through a forbidden door-map that everyone sensible believes was burned centuries ago.
input.character_description: A quick-witted sky-courier who smuggles refugees off worlds marked for erasure; his recurring trouble is a blood-debt to the very tyrant whose work he's undoing.
input.character_details: FATE Accelerated, DM-managed. High Concept: Quick-Witted Sky-Courier of the Planar Routes. Trouble: Blood-Debt to the Tyrant Whose Work I'm Undoing. Extra Aspect: No Door Is Closed If You Hit It Fast Enough. Approaches: Flashy +3; Quick +2, Clever +2; Sneaky +1, Careful +1; Forceful +0. Stunt defaults: one starting stunt may emerge in play when Roan does something especially signature; refresh/fate points managed behind the scenes.
input.campaign_detail: Fully custom one-shot. Heroic planar pulp with cliffhangers, impossible skies, desperate rescues, tyrannical cosmic bureaucracy, doomed worlds, brass-and-starlight vehicles, and lovingly ridiculous hazards. The Red Auditor's erasures are performed through official cosmic writs, seals, inspectors, and execution engines; play the villain as terrifyingly lawful rather than merely cruel. DM-only twist: Roan's blood-debt is not just leverage — it is part of the Red Auditor's authority to find him across planes, and it can also be turned into the key that invalidates one erasure decree if Roan dares spend it. Keep the one-shot moving toward a daring evacuation/rescue through the forbidden door-map, with at least one midair chase, one social gambit, and one impossible doorway.
input.handoff_note: Sam came in with a very clear concept and asked for Full Setup but also supplied most key choices at once: pulpy planar-adventure, FATE Accelerated as a light system, The Chronicler as DM personality, adult table, heroic pulp mood, and a tight one-shot. Sam specifically said: "My character is Roan Calloway, a quick-witted sky-courier who smuggles refugees off worlds marked for erasure; his recurring trouble is a blood-debt to the very tyrant whose work he's undoing. Flashy daring is his strongest approach." They also explicitly want the rules run behind the scenes — do not ask them to track dice, aspects, fate points, or spends unless they request it. Tone cues: high-energy heroic pulp, surprise them with the rest, make Roan feel competent, wanted, and morally driven. I promised cliffhangers, impossible skies, desperate rescues, tyrannical cosmic bureaucracy, and a few lovingly ridiculous pulp hazards.
input.opening_scene: Open with Roan Calloway alone on the swaying maintenance catwalk of his battered sky-courier vessel, repainting a false registry number onto the hull while refugees sleep in the hidden hold and the planar dawn rises green over a world not yet marked for erasure.

result.text:
The seal is set.
The first door is about to remember your name.
All right, Sam — Roan Calloway is loaded into the engine, the planar sky is waiting, and The Chronicler has the stage. I’ll step aside now. Good luck out there, courier. ---
